_meta.purpose: Single source of truth for pages/support/how-to-get-help.html (the page that absorbs the deleted zoom-sessions.html). Drives a single-card / sections-within layout via render_how_to_get_help.py.

pageTitle: How to Get Help | IS2053 Programming I

quickRef.label: Quick Reference
quickRef.intro_html: Stuck on an assignment? Confused about a concept? Here’s how to get help. Try them in order — most questions get answered fastest through Discord, with Calendly available when you need focused 1-on-1 time.

channels.label: Discord
channels.value_html:
Fastest response time — classmates and instructor both monitor
Post in your module’s #module-N-help channel

channels.label: Book a 1-on-1
channels.value_html:
15-minute focused session via Calendly
Book on Calendly →

channels.label: Tuesday Office Hours
channels.value_html:
Tuesdays at 6:00 PM CT — drop-in for general questions. Not 1-on-1.
Join the Zoom Session →

channels.label: Email
channels.value_html:
For private matters — grades, accommodations, personal circumstances
john.newsom@utsa.edu

discord.label: Discord

sections.heading: Why Discord?
sections.body_html: The class Discord is the fastest way to get help. Post your question in your module’s #module-N-help channel and classmates or the instructor will respond — often within minutes. You also get the benefit of seeing how other students have solved similar problems.

sections.heading: How to Ask a Good Question
sections.body_html:
The better you describe your problem, the faster you’ll get an answer.

  • Describe what you’re trying to do — what is the goal of this code?
  • Share the error message — paste it in a code block so it’s readable
  • Show what you’ve already tried — this helps skip suggestions you’ve already ruled out
  • Be specific — “it doesn’t work” doesn’t help us help you

callout.title: Using Code Blocks
callout.body_html: Wrap your code and error messages in triple backticks so Discord formats them correctly. Type ```, paste your code, then ``` again. This makes your question much easier to read.

discord.closing_html: View Discord Info and Join Link →

officeHours.label: Tuesday Office Hours
officeHours._styleNote: Section uses cyan rather than blue because lc-named-section--blue isn't in the modifier set; cyan reads as the closest 'cool / communication' tone.

sections.heading: When & Where
sections.body_html:
Tuesdays at 6:00 PM CT
Open office hours — drop in for general questions. This is not 1-on-1 help. If you need someone to walk through your specific code with you, book a Calendly slot instead — you’ll get a focused 15-minute session that’s actually built for that.

Join Zoom Session →

sections.heading: What to Expect
sections.body_html:
Tuesday office hours are drop-in, not a lecture. There’s no structured content — what comes up depends on what people bring.

  • Open discussion of the current lab or BookEx assignment
  • General questions that benefit the whole class
  • Announcements and reminders about what’s coming up
  • A space to compare notes with classmates who joined

Sessions are recorded and posted to Canvas after each Tuesday. Camera optional — no pressure to be on video.

callout.title: When to Use Tuesday Office Hours
callout.body_html: If your question is specific to your code, Discord or Calendly will serve you better. Tuesday office hours are best for questions where the answer helps more than one person — concept reviews, “is this the right approach?” questions, and general assignment orientation.

sections.heading: When to Book
sections.body_html:
Need dedicated one-on-one time? Book a 15-minute appointment through Calendly. This is great for complex debugging, career questions, or anything that needs focused attention outside the Tuesday session.

Book an Appointment on Calendly →

sections.heading: Good Reasons to Book
sections.body_html:
• You’ve been stuck on the same bug for more than an hour
  • You want to talk through your overall approach before writing more code
  • You have a grade question or concern you’d rather discuss privately
  • You want to chat about Python beyond the coursework, career paths, or next steps

callout.title: Come Prepared
callout.body_html: 15 minutes goes fast. Have your code open, your error message ready, and a specific question in mind. The more targeted your question, the more we can accomplish in the session.

sections.heading: When to Email
sections.body_html: Email is best for private matters: grades, accommodations, personal circumstances, or anything you don’t want to discuss publicly. For coding questions, Discord or Tuesday office hours will get you a faster answer.

callout.title: Pro Tip
callout.body_html: Include IS2053 in your subject line so your email stands out and gets to you faster. For example: IS2053 — Question about Lab 3-2 submission.
